summary: Finished matching the player stale-move module by replacing asm/melee/pl/plstale.s with matched C in src/melee/pl/plstale.c, marking plstale.c as Matching, and updating symbols/callsites to descriptive plStale names. The PR also clarified stale-move data names and signedness across Fighter, Item, and StaleMoveTable fields, especially attack_instance handling.
what_changed: Deleted the 322-line asm implementation asm/melee/pl/plstale.s and switched obj_files.mk from asm/melee/pl/plstale.s.o to src/melee/pl/plstale.c.o.
Changed configure.py so melee/pl/plstale.c is built as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).

Implemented and/or renamed plstale functions in C: plStale_InitAttackInstance, plStale_ResetStaleMoveTableForPlayer, plStale_IncrementAttackInstance, plStale_UpdateStaleMovesFromFighter, and plStale_UpdateStaleMovesFromItem.
Renamed the global u16 unk_804D6478 to staleAttackInstance and preserved the wraparound behavior that skips zero by resetting to 1.

Implemented stale table update logic for fighter and item hits: skip self/owner matches, skip attack_id 1, reject duplicate attack_id plus attack_instance pairs, write into current_index, then wrap current_index from 9 back to 0.
Updated assembly callsites in ft_0881.s, ft_0D14.s, ftcoll.s, it_2725.s, and plbonus.s to call the new descriptive plStale symbols.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to replace address-based plStale_80037xxx names with descriptive names while preserving addresses and sizes.

Clarified stale-move-related struct fields: Fighter x2068_attackID became s32 and x206C became u16 x206C_attack_instance; Item xD8C_attackInstance became xD8C_attack_instance; StaleMoveTable entries became u16 move_id and u16 attack_instance.
Adjusted ft_0881.c and ft_0881.h to use u32 move_id parameters and casts when comparing against the s32 fighter attack ID.
smart_moves: The PR did not just match the C file; it also propagated meaningful symbol names into headers, symbols.txt, and existing assembly callsites, making future cross-module work easier to search.
The stale table fields were renamed based on observed behavior: the second halfword is treated as an attack instance, not num_action_states.

The implementation used simple for loops over the 10 stale slots and a ternary wrap for current_index, capturing the repeated assembly comparisons in readable C without preserving the unrolled asm shape.
Using GET_FIGHTER, GET_ITEM, and ftLib_80086960 made the item-owner stale update path express the real object relationships instead of leaving raw offset access in asm.
The build/config updates were complete: obj_files.mk, configure.py, and symbols.txt all moved together, avoiding a half-matched state.
decomp_lessons: When a module becomes matching, update both build systems represented in this repo slice: configure.py's Object(Matching/NonMatching) entry and obj_files.mk's source-vs-asm object path.
Descriptive symbol names should be applied consistently to C headers, symbols.txt, and remaining asm callers so later decomp work can follow semantic names across mixed C/asm boundaries.
A repeated unrolled assembly pattern over a fixed-size table may match cleanly as a normal C for loop if the compiler settings and types line up.
Field signedness matters for matching: this PR changed stale table entries to u16 while retaining s32 for attack IDs, and used explicit casts in ft_0881.c comparisons.
Stale-move table matching depends on recognizing the pair identity of move_id plus attack_instance; storing only the move ID would not reflect the assembly behavior.
Global counters that must not become zero can be modeled directly with increment-then-if-zero-reset logic; here the stale attack instance returns the previous value and resets zero to one.
naming_conventions: Descriptive plStale function names use UpperCamel after the module prefix; struct fields in this area use existing offset prefixes plus snake_case semantic suffixes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the entire plstale.s text/sbss assembly file after matching all five functions in C.
Preserved exact public symbol addresses and sizes in config/GALE01/symbols.txt while replacing only the names.
Used a 10-iteration loop to replace unrolled stale-table duplicate checks in both fighter and item update functions.
Modeled ring-buffer wrap as current_index == 9 ? 0 : current_index + 1.
Used u16 for attack_instance storage and staleAttackInstance, matching lhz/sth behavior visible in the deleted assembly.
Used explicit casts in ft_0881.c where u32 move_id is compared to s32 x2068_attackID, likely to preserve codegen after signedness cleanup.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining stale-move fields or comments using old names like num_action_states or x206C.
Look for other modules that still call plStale symbols only from asm and could now be decompiled with the descriptive prototypes.
Audit StaleMoveTable::total_attack_count usage; this PR did not change it beyond nearby struct typing.
Check whether fighter and item stale-update logic can later share a helper without disrupting matching, since the C bodies are structurally similar.
